notes: Conservative v0.x floors. The seed corpus is small (6 servers / 57 tools), so per-rule numbers are statistically noisy; only aggregate, micro-averaged floors are gated. minPrecision is the load-bearing false-flag guard. Floors are set by the one-false-flag budget: after a change lands and the measured state on the committed corpus is TP/FP, minPrecision is the largest 0.05-multiple <= TP/(TP+FP+1), so the gate tolerates exactly one noise FP at the current positive count before failing. Floors only ratchet up as the corpus grows; a downward move is a ratified exception with a written reason. Current basis (2026-07-06, corpus = 6 servers / 57 tools, measured after the precision-recovery work): 8 TP / 0 FP / 3 FN => precision 100.0%, recall 72.7%, F0.5 93.0%; budget 8/(8+0+1)=88.9% => minPrecision 0.85. Recompute at every corpus expansion.
